description: §49.4.4 — `let x = while (...) { ... lift ... }` puts a `while` in an expression position WITHOUT the §49.6 `lift`-accumulator pattern (the body lifts, but the value is bound by direct assignment rather than consumed via `~`), firing E-LOOP-007. Twins with loop-007-while-as-expr-no-lift-pos, whose body does NOT lift and still fires — together they pin that the trigger is the expression-position BINDING of the `while`, not the presence of a `lift` token. FIX HISTORY (ss75->fix, 2026-07-16): checkWhileAsExpr formerly required a `\blift\b` token in the body (type-system.ts, a workaround for an ast-builder misparse that folded a following while-stmt into a preceding decl's init), so the SPEC's own no-lift §49.4.4/§49.9 example stayed silent — an under-fire hole. That misparse no longer reproduces at HEAD, so the narrowing was dropped; the no-lift twin now guards the closed hole. W-PROGRAM-001 co-fires incidentally (no `<program>` shell); not asserted.
rationale: §49.4.4: 'A bare `while` statement SHALL NOT be used as an expression. The compiler SHALL reject any attempt to use a `while` statement in an expression position (e.g., `let x = while (...) { ... }`) without the `lift` accumulator pattern (§49.6). The error is E-LOOP-007.' §49.4.4 further fixes the sole exception: 'A `while` statement in a `lift`-accumulating context (§49.6) is valid as an expression position assignment target via the `~` accumulator. This is the only exception.' §49.6.1 fixes that pattern's shape: the `while` stays a STATEMENT and the accumulated values are consumed by a following `let result = ~`. This case uses `lift` but binds via direct assignment, not via `~`, so it is NOT the §49.6 pattern and NOT the sole exception — it is an expression-position `while` and SHALL be rejected. §34 and §49.9 both register E-LOOP-007 at severity Error. WORDING (resolved ss75->fix): the one-line rows at §34 and §49.10 formerly compressed the condition to '`while` used as an expression without `lift`/`~`', which read literally would NOT fire on this case (its body does contain `lift`). Those rows — plus the §49.9 definition header/prose — were reworded to '`while` bound as a value ... the §49.6.1 statement-`while` + `let result = ~` accumulator is the sole exception', matching the normative §49.4.4 prose, which conditions on the full §49.6 `lift`+`~` accumulator PATTERN rather than the bare presence of a `lift` token.
